description: Test if geometric N_eff exponent (slope of log N_eff vs log(K-1)) is architecture-universal at ~0.93. Pythia-160m measured slope=0.934 (DBpedia) and 0.918 (Banking77). Testing 3 additional architectures.
hypothesis: The geometric N_eff exponent is architecture-universal: each architecture gives slope in [0.85, 1.00] when measured from pure centroid geometry.

pre_registered_criteria.PR3_cross_arch_cv: CV of slopes across all 4 architectures (including pythia-160m baseline) < 10%
pre_registered_criteria.PASS: PR1 and PR2 pass for >=3 of 3 new architectures, PR3 passes

motivation: Currently only pythia-160m has been tested. If slope~0.93 is universal across architectures, this strengthens the claim that geometric N_eff competition is a property of the classification geometry, not a pythia-specific artifact.
